title: Blockade of Strait of Hormuz Commences; Hezbollah Urges Lebanon to Withdraw from Israel Normalization Talks
detail: The blockade of the Strait of Hormuz formally commenced on April 14, 2026, marking a critical escalation in the Iran conflict. Iran's naval forces began enforcing a full closure of the strategic waterway, which carries approximately 20% of global oil supply. Simultaneously, Hezbollah issued a public call for Lebanon to withdraw from normalization talks with Israel, warning that any Lebanese diplomatic rapprochement with Tel Aviv would undermine the broader resistance axis. The dual developments are covered as part of the escalating Middle East crisis following the collapse of Islamabad peace talks.

media.caption: Hormuz blockade commences as Hezbollah urges Lebanon to pull from Israel normalization talks
